summary: Dan Shapiro shares inspiration images of Japanese step tansu chests from various furniture retailers, considering one for an upcoming woodworking or maker project. The brief post is essentially a mood board of tansu designs he finds appealing. He acknowledges his growing backlog of projects but embraces it, noting that the design phase is enjoyable in itself.
key_points: Shapiro curates reference images of step tansu chests from multiple Asian furniture retailers
The post reflects his hands-on maker mentality — always planning the next physical project
He openly acknowledges accumulating a backlog of projects but sees designing as its own reward
money_quotes: Yeah, I realize I'm accumulating quite a backlog of projects. That's OK, designing is half the fun.
key_insight: For a dedicated maker, the design and inspiration phase is as rewarding as the build itself.
